sections.paragraph:
to be narrow, straitened, compressed. (Arabic ضاق Med. Ye. Eth. ጠዊቅ፡ to straiten, to compress, kindred roots are עוּק, and also עָנַק, חָנַק, and those connected with them.)

Hiphil הֵצִיק-

(1) to straiten, to press upon any one, followed by a dat. and acc., Deu 28:53, seqq.; Jer 19:9 Job 32:18 specially to straiten a city by siege, Isa 29:7. Part. מֵצִיק oppressor, Isa 51:13.

(2) to urge any one with prayers, with an acc., Jud. 4:17 with a dative, 16:16 .

Derivatives, צֹוק, צוּקָה, מוּצַק, מָצֹוק, מְצוּקָה.

II.

צוק

-

(1) i.q. יָצַק to pour, Job 29:6 28:2 , אֶבֶן יָצוּק נְחוּשָׁה “and the stone is poured out (to make) brass,” i.e. they melt the ore into brass. Metaph. Isa 26:16, צָקוּן לַחַשׁ “they pour out a prayer” (צָקוּן, Milra, is pret. Kal with Nun paragogic, for צָ֫קוּ).

(2) i.q. הִצִּיק to set up; whence מָצוּק a column.
